item.title: Switzerland and Bosnia-Herzegovina locked at 0-0 at half-time
item.description: Switzerland and Bosnia-Herzegovina are locked at 0-0 at half-time in their FIFA World Cup Group B clash. The match at Los Angeles Stadium remains evenly poised, with both sides yet to find the breakthrough as they head into the second half.
item.content:encoded:
Switzerland and Bosnia-Herzegovina are locked at 0-0 at half-time in their FIFA World Cup Group B clash.

The match at Los Angeles Stadium remains evenly poised, with both sides yet to find the breakthrough as they head into the second half.

Switzerland entered the match after a 1-1 draw with Qatar and are making their 13th FIFA World Cup appearance. Their best performance at the tournament remains reaching the quarter-finals.

Bosnia-Herzegovina, meanwhile, are making only their second FIFA World Cup appearance. They opened their Group B campaign with a 1-1 draw against Canada and are chasing their first-ever World Cup victory.

Meanwhile, South Africa fought back to earn a 1-1 draw against the Czech Republic in their FIFA World Cup clash this morning.

Canada will take on Qatar at 10am, while Mexico will meet South Korea at 1pm.

item.title: Hurricanes and Chiefs set for Super Rugby Pacific title showdown
item.description: The tickets for the final were sold out in 15 minutes, guaranteeing a packed stadium and a vibrant atmosphere in Te Whanganui-ā-Tara for what promises to be an unforgettable championship clash. The Hurricanes and Chiefs will meet in at 7.05pm tomorrow.
item.content:encoded:
The tickets for the final were sold out in 15 minutes, guaranteeing a packed stadium and a vibrant atmosphere in Te Whanganui-ā-Tara for what promises to be an unforgettable championship clash.

The Hurricanes and Chiefs will meet in at 7.05pm tomorrow.

item.title: Late penalty earns South Africa 1-1 draw against Czech Republic
item.description: South Africa fought back to earn a 1-1 draw against the Czech Republic in their FIFA World Cup clash this morning. The Czech Republic made the perfect start, taking the lead after just five minutes through Michal Sadilek.
item.content:encoded:
South Africa fought back to earn a 1-1 draw against the Czech Republic in their FIFA World Cup clash this morning.

The Czech Republic made the perfect start, taking the lead after just five minutes through Michal Sadilek.

South Africa struggled to create chances and did not register a shot on target until the 74th minute.

They were awarded a late penalty, with Teboho Mokoena converting from the spot in the 83rd minute to secure a valuable draw.

South Africa began their FIFA World Cup campaign with a 2-0 loss to Mexico, while the Czech Republic suffered a narrow 2-1 defeat to South Korea in their opening Group A match.

South Africa will now take on South Korea at 1pm next Thursday in their final Group A match, while the Czech Republic will face Mexico at the same time on the same day.

Meanwhile, Switzerland will face Bosnia and Herzegovina in the next hour, Canada will take on Qatar at 10am, while Mexico will meet South Korea at 1pm.

item.title: Tavua captain Gabrieli Ratu and player Jone Lewenilovo sanctioned for abusing match official
item.description: Tavua Rugby Union captain Gabrieli Ratu and player Jone Lewenilovo have been sanctioned for one year and nearly eight months respe

item.title: SJSS hoping to defend U-19 netball tittle
item.description: Defending Fiji Secondary Schools Netball champions Saint Joseph's Secondary School are aiming to retain their Under-19 title at this year's Fiji Secondary Schools Netball Tournament. The Saint Joseph Under-19 side did not feature in the opening round of competition at the Suva Grammar School grounds yesterday.
item.content:encoded:
Defending Fiji Secondary Schools Netball champions Saint Joseph's Secondary School are aiming to retain their Under-19 title at this year's Fiji Secondary Schools Netball Tournament.

The Saint Joseph Under-19 side did not feature in the opening round of competition at the Suva Grammar School grounds yesterday.

Meanwhile, after establishing itself as one of Fiji’s leading academic institutions for more than a decade, Jai Narayan College is now setting its sights on sporting success, with hopes of adding trophies to its achievements.

The school is participating in the Fiji Secondary Schools Netball Tournament for the first time, reflecting its commitment to the holistic development of its students.

School teacher and tournament organiser Master Pio Kerewaqa said that while Jai Narayan College has enjoyed significant academic success over the years, sporting accolades have been largely absent from the school’s trophy cabinet.

Kerewaqa says the school is determined to build on its strong academic reputation by encouraging greater participation in sports, beginning with netball.

He emphasised that sport plays a crucial role in developing students’ character, teaching valuable lessons in teamwork, discipline, leadership, and resilience that extend beyond the classroom.

According to Kerewaqa, the school’s involvement in the tournament forms part of a broader effort to provide students with a well-rounded education that promotes both academic excellence and physical development.

Through its participation in netball, Jai Narayan College hopes to cultivate a stronger sporting culture and celebrate success both in the classroom and on the court.

In the Under-17 grade, Jai Narayan College put up a strong performance against Saint Joseph Secondary School but went down 18-14.

Elsewhere, in the Under-15 division, Suva Grammar School defeated DAV Girls College 18-7.

In another Under-17 match, Saint Joseph Secondary School overcame Yat Sen Secondary School 16-11.

The Saint Joseph Secondary School Under-19 team did not play yesterday.

item.title: Ba ready for Fiji FACT challenge as coach backs new-look squad
item.description: Ba FC head coach Ravinesh Kumar says his side is well prepared and ready for the challenge ahead as the 2026 BiC Fiji FACT gets un

item.title: Vodafone Fiji and FNRL mark 20-year partnership with launch of 2026 Vodafone Cup
item.description: Vodafone Fiji and the Fiji National Rugby League celebrated their 20 years of partnership today, as the launch of the 2026 Vodafone Cup competition was held at the HFC Bank Stadium. FNRL Chairman Rajen Singh says what began as a commitment to support rugby league has grown into one of the longest-standing sporting partnerships in Fiji, and this is one of the most enduring relationships anywhere in
item.content:encoded:
Vodafone Fiji and the Fiji National Rugby League celebrated their 20 years of partnership today, as the launch of the 2026 Vodafone Cup competition was held at the HFC Bank Stadium.

FNRL Chairman Rajen Singh says what began as a commitment to support rugby league has grown into one of the longest-standing sporting partnerships in Fiji, and this is one of the most enduring relationships anywhere in the Pacific.

Singh says he wants to acknowledge Vodafone Fiji for not only supporting rugby league, but investing in people's lives and giving young people hope, creating opportunities and providing pathways over two decades.

The Chairman says 44 teams will be participating in this year's Vodafone Cup with 11 women's team.

The competition kicks off this Saturday.

item.description: Colombia made a winning return to the FIFA World Cup by defeating debutants Uzbekistan 3-1.After failing to qualify for the 2022 c

item.title: Harry Kane scores twice as England defeats Croatia 4-2
item.description: England has made a winning start to their FIFA World Cup campaign after defeating Croatia 4-2 in an entertaining Group L encounter

item.title: Portugal and DR Congo play out 1-1 draw in Group K clash
item.description: Portugal and DR Congo played out a 1-1 draw in their opening FIFA World Cup Group K match this morning.Portugal got off to a flyin
